topic: An athlete is asked to switch positions
raw_output:
[story 1]

The coach’s voice cut through the sterile air of the locker room, sharp and utterly devoid of preamble. "Marcus, we're shifting you to the center position for the next competition. It’s a change of pace." Marcus stared at his reflection in the mirrored wall, the familiar lines of his specialized gear suddenly feeling alien. He blinked slowly, processing the instruction. Center? He was a flanker, built for speed on the wings. A slow, almost imperceptible tremor ran through his hands as he adjusted his tape. "Center?" he managed, the word sounding strangely hollow in his own ears. He looked down at his feet, suddenly feeling unbalanced, as if gravity had shifted its pull.

[story 2]

"Just try it, Leo," the trainer urged, clapping him on the shoulder with too much enthusiasm. "It'll open up some new angles for the team." Leo stood still, his jaw tight. He could feel a strange tightening in his chest, a knot forming just beneath his ribs. He took a deep, shaky breath, the air catching in his throat. He glanced around the room—the familiar faces of his teammates seemed distant, blurred. He shuffled his weight from one foot to the other, a hesitant, almost clumsy movement. "I... I don't know what you mean," he mumbled, his voice barely audible, focusing intently on a scuff mark on the floor, desperate for something concrete to anchor him.

[story 3]

The announcement hung in the gymnasium like a heavy curtain. "Sarah, you'll be taking the pivot role. It requires a completely different kind of stamina." Sarah felt a sudden, cold rush wash over her skin. Her mind raced, trying to map out the physical requirements of the new role, but the images were all jumbled, nonsensical. She gripped the edge of her water bottle so hard her knuckles turned white. When the coach looked her way, she met his gaze, but her eyes seemed to drift past him, fixed on a point somewhere far beyond the bleachers. "Pivot?" she repeated, her tone flat, a slight hesitation betraying the sudden vacuum where her usual confidence used to reside.
